testPurpose.objective: Verify five independent users can create, join, retain identity, complete one baseline Avalon game through legal visible actions, keep private assignment information isolated, and reach one consistent explained terminal result.

successCriteria.description: Every tab proves its own identity before play, and a one-tab reload preserves that identity without changing peer tabs.
The host applies the recommended five-player deck, standard clockwise leaders, no result delay, and disabled expansions through visible controls, and all tabs render the same settings.
No tab or isolated player agent receives another player's private assignment information or player-associated quest card before the terminal state.
Visible proposal, team-vote, quest, leader-rotation, and any naturally reached assassination controls accept only Adapter-legal owning-player actions.
All five tabs reach the same normalized terminal result and public quest history, regardless of which side naturally wins.

testPurpose.recommendationRationale: The visible five-player recommended deck is the smallest official-style core game. Standard clockwise rotation removes an extra leadership choice, no-delay avoids an unnecessary wait, and disabled expansions isolate the first certification attempt to the baseline loop without prescribing a winner.
